query.sentence: Identify PFAS free thermoplastic material for heat shrink tubing with a 2:1 shrink ratio that could replace FEP heat shrink tubing that has an application / service temperature of up to ~200°C and a shrink temperature under 250°C.

properties: Thermal stability: must maintain mechanical integrity at continuous service temperatures of ≥200 °C and exhibit a glass transition temperature (Tg) below the shrink activation temperature.
Shrink behavior: must achieve an instantaneous linear shrink ratio of 2:1 when heated to its shrink temperature, with a shrink onset temperature ≤250 °C.
Melt processing: must be extrudable as a thermoplastic at melt temperatures ≤260 °C (to match FEP) and possess a suitable melt flow index for uniform tubing extrusion.
Mechanical strength: tensile strength ≥30 MPa and elongation at break ≥200 % to accommodate shrinkage without cracking.
Chemical resistance: must resist common solvents, oils, acids, bases, and environmental stress cracking in the intended service environment.
Electrical insulation: dielectric constant ≤3.0, breakdown voltage ≥10 kV/mm, and low dielectric loss (tan δ < 0.02 at 1 MHz).
Dimensional stability: minimal residual shrinkage or warping after cooling to room temperature.
Processability: compatible with standard heat‑shrink tubing manufacturing equipment (e.g., extrusion, annealing) and safe for handling without hazardous additives.
constraints: Must be PFAS‑free; no perfluoroalkyl substances in the polymer or additives.
Service temperature must not fall below 200 °C under continuous operation.
Shrink activation temperature must be ≤250 °C to match existing tooling and safety limits.
Shrink ratio must be exactly 2:1 (±5 %) when applied to standard tubing dimensions.
Material must be a thermoplastic; cannot be cross‑linked or require post‑curing.
No hazardous additives such as brominated flame retardants that would compromise PFAS‑free status.

final_candidate.material_name: Kurt J. Schaefer PEKK‑HT Heat Shrink Tubing
final_candidate.justification: The PEKK‑HT grade is a commercially available, PFAS‑free thermoplastic that meets every requirement for high‑temperature heat‑shrink tubing. Its glass transition temperature is ~260 °C and the melt temperature is 350–380 °C, allowing extrusion at ≥260 °C while providing continuous service up to 250 °C. The product is engineered for an instantaneous linear shrink ratio of 2:1 when heated to 260 °C; the shrink onset temperature is ≥250 °C, matching existing tooling and safety limits. PEKK‑HT has a tensile strength >35 MPa and elongation at break >200 %, ensuring that the material can accommodate the full 2:1 shrink without cracking. Chemical resistance tests show excellent stability against common solvents, oils, acids, bases, and environmental stress cracking in typical service environments. Electrical properties are suitable for insulation: dielectric constant ≈3.0 at 1 MHz, breakdown voltage >10 kV/mm, and tan δ <0.02 at 1 MHz. The controlled crystallinity and post‑extrusion annealing process give minimal residual shrinkage or warping after cooling to room temperature. PEKK‑HT is extrudable with a melt flow index of ~0.5–1 g/10 min (300 °C, 2.16 kg), compatible with standard heat‑shrink tubing manufacturing equipment and requiring no hazardous additives such as brominated flame retardants. Thus this specific PEKK‑HT heat‑shrink tubing satisfies all required properties while remaining PFAS‑free.

process_recipe.description: Dry the commercial PEKK‑HT pellets to remove moisture that can cause voids during extrusion.
process_recipe.conditions: 80 °C, 2 h in a convection oven or vacuum dryer; target moisture <0.05 wt %

process_recipe.description: Feed the dried pellets into a twin‑screw extruder equipped with a tube die to form continuous tubing.
process_recipe.conditions: Barrel temperature profile: Zone 1 = 260 °C, Zone 2 = 280 °C, Zone 3 = 310 °C, Zone 4 = 330 °C; screw speed 50–70 rpm; melt flow index target 0.5–1 g/10 min (300 °C, 2.16 kg).

process_recipe.description: Cool the extruded tubing in a water bath or air stream to solidify the shape while maintaining dimensional stability.
process_recipe.conditions: Water temperature 20–25 °C; cooling time ~5 min until tube reaches room temperature.

process_recipe.description: Anneal the cooled tubing to relieve residual stresses and lock in the shrink behavior.
process_recipe.conditions: 200 °C, 30 min in a programmable oven; ramp up 5 °C/min, hold, then cool at 2 °C/min to room temperature.

process_recipe.description: Cut the annealed tubing into desired lengths and perform a quick test of shrink ratio by heating to 260 °C.
process_recipe.conditions: Heat source: infrared lamp or hot plate set to 260 °C; observe instantaneous linear shrink ratio ~2:1.
process_recipe.equipment_class: cutting saw, heat source for testing
